name: Canarian Spanish

allophone_rules.notes: Canarian /x/ (⟨j⟩ and ⟨g⟩ before front vowel) is a glottal/pharyngeal aspirate [h], never the Castilian velar [x] — a defining Atlantic feature shared with Andalusian and Caribbean Spanish: cojo [ˈkoho], trabajo [tɾaˈβaho], coge [ˈkohe] (Hualde 2005, The Sounds of Spanish, ch. on Canarian and Caribbean varieties; Zamora Vicente 1960, Dialectología española §El español de Canarias). Only the /x/ phoneme is rewritten; intervocalic /ɡ/→[ɣ] (which is a different phoneme) is untouched.

notes: Canarian Spanish (Islas Canarias). Systematic seseo (no /θ/), yeísmo, coda /s/ aspiration (less extreme than Andalusian), partial /x/ aspiration. Strong historical connections to Andalusian and Caribbean Spanish. Conservative affricate [tʃ] preservation. -d deletion in participial -ado.

sources.author: Penny, R.
sources.title: A History of the Spanish Language

location.notes: the Canary Islands — the archipelago the variety is spoken in. [Wikidata Q5813 P625]
